questions: Why does `hlx tickets artifacts` ignore the `--run <runId>` flag?
Why does requesting a missing step artifact via `hlx tickets artifact` trigger a noisy Node assertion failure?
What existing patterns in the codebase should the fix follow?

answers.answer: `cmdTicketsArtifacts` in `src/tickets/artifacts.ts` (line 17) accepts only `(config: HxConfig, ticketId: string)` ŌĆö it has no `args` parameter, so it cannot read any CLI flags. The subcommand router at `src/tickets/index.ts` line 79 calls `cmdTicketsArtifacts(config, ticketId)` without passing `rest`. The `hxFetch` call at line 18 uses no `queryParams`, so `runId` is never sent to the server even though the server already supports it.
evidence: src/tickets/artifacts.ts:17 ŌĆö function signature: `(config: HxConfig, ticketId: string): Promise<void>`
src/tickets/artifacts.ts:18 ŌĆö hxFetch call: `hxFetch(config, `/tickets/${ticketId}/artifacts`, { basePath: "/api" })` ŌĆö no queryParams
src/tickets/index.ts:79 ŌĆö router: `await cmdTicketsArtifacts(config, ticketId)` ŌĆö rest not passed (compare line 85 which passes rest to cmdTicketsArtifact)

answers.answer: When `hxFetch` receives a 404 response, it builds an error message via `buildErrorMessage` (reading response body as text) and throws `new Error(message)`. This propagates to the global catch at `src/index.ts:95-97` which logs the message and calls `process.exit(1)`. The `artifact.ts` command at lines 42-45 has no try-catch around its `hxFetch` call, unlike `bundle.ts` (lines 68-71) which wraps individual artifact fetches in try-catch for graceful failure. The exact assertion failure mechanism is not determinable from static analysis alone, but the fix is clear: handle 404 at the call site in `artifact.ts` with a try-catch providing a clean user-facing message, preventing the raw HTTP error from reaching the global handler.
evidence: src/tickets/artifact.ts:42-45 ŌĆö hxFetch call with no error handling
src/lib/http.ts:102-103 ŌĆö non-retryable status throws: `const message = await buildErrorMessage(response); throw new Error(message)`
src/lib/http.ts:33-34 ŌĆö buildErrorMessage reads body: `response.text()` then formats `HTTP ${status} ${statusText} ŌĆö ${text.slice(0, 500)}`
src/tickets/bundle.ts:68-71 ŌĆö reference graceful pattern: try-catch with console.error warning

answers.answer: Two established patterns exist. For --run flag support: `artifact.ts` lines 29-40 reads `--run` via `getFlag(args, '--run')` and passes it as a query parameter. For error handling: `bundle.ts` lines 68-71 wraps `hxFetch` calls in try-catch and prints a warning on failure. The `flags.ts` utility provides `getFlag(args, flag)` which returns the value or undefined.
evidence: src/tickets/artifact.ts:29-40 ŌĆö --run flag pattern: getFlag ŌåÆ if absent, fetch ticket detail ŌåÆ fallback to currentRun?.id ?? runs[0]?.id
src/tickets/bundle.ts:68-71 ŌĆö graceful error pattern: `catch { console.error('Warning: Could not fetch artifact ...') }`
src/lib/flags.ts:5-8 ŌĆö getFlag API: `getFlag(args, flag)` returns `string | undefined`

statement: Two independent defects exist in the helix-cli artifact commands. (1) `cmdTicketsArtifacts` in `src/tickets/artifacts.ts` lacks an `args` parameter, and the router in `src/tickets/index.ts` does not pass CLI args to it, so the `--run` flag is never read or forwarded as a query parameter despite the server already supporting `runId`. (2) `cmdTicketsArtifact` in `src/tickets/artifact.ts` has no error handling around its `hxFetch` call at lines 42-45; a 404 response for a missing step artifact propagates as a thrown error through the global handler, producing a noisy error message and potentially triggering Node assertion failures on exit. Both fixes follow well-established patterns already present in sibling commands within the same codebase.
